concerns.text: Tasks T042-T045 (Rule Runner simulation) introduce a new data-flow dependency (synthetic data generation) that is not present in the upstream spec or plan. The plan explicitly limits scope to MOND/NFW fitting on SPARC data. These tasks consume 'Rule Runner' output (T043) which has no producer in the approved spec, violating the 'Producer before consumer' principle and introducing unapproved scope creep that breaks the independent shippability of US2.

Task T043 generates 'synthetic rotation curve candidates' to be consumed by T044. This creates a data artifact (synthetic data) that is not defined in the data-model.md or spec.md (which mandates SPARC data only). The consumer (T044) cannot exist without this unapproved producer, and the producer violates Constitution Principle VI (Observational Data Integrity) by introducing non-SPARC data into the pipeline.

Task T035 (Sensitivity Report) depends on T026 (Sensitivity Analysis). While the dependency is logically sound, T035 is listed after T042-T045 in the task list, creating a visual ordering confusion where the report generation appears to depend on the unapproved Rule Runner tasks. Re-order T035 to immediately follow T026 to clarify the data flow within US2.

Tasks T021 (MOND) and T022 (NFW) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T023 (Fitting Engine) depends on the *output* of both. While the implementation of the models can be parallel, the task list implies they are independent units. Ensure the description clarifies that T021/T022 produce *modules* (code artifacts) that T023 consumes, not that they produce data artifacts consumed by each other. The current [P] tag is technically correct for code generation but risks confusion regarding data flow if interpreted as data production.
